gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/107)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/107)
text: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ أَوَّلُ كُلِّ كِتَابٍ نَزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ فَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ فَلَا تُبَالِي أَلَّا تَسْتَعِيذَ وَ إِذَا قَرَأْتَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ سَتَرَتْكَ فِيمَا بَيْنَ السَّمَاءِ وَ الْأَرْضِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ali Bin Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Abbad Bin Yaqoub, from Amro Bin Musa’ab, from Furat Bin Ahnaf,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I heard him<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The beginning of every Book Revealed from the sky was ‘In the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> the Beneficent, the Merciful’. So when you recited ‘In the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> the Beneficent, the Merciful’, so it does not matter if you do not seek Refuge (recite ‘I seek Refuge with Allah<sup>azwj</sup> from the Pelted Satan<sup>la</sup>’); and when you recite ‘In the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> the Beneficent, the Merciful, it would veil you regarding what is between the sky and the earth’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ali ibn al-Hassan ibn Ali from ‘Abbad ibn Ya‘qub from ‘Amr ibn Mus’ab from Furat ibn Ahnaf who has said the following: “I heard abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘Every book that has come from the heavens begins with “In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful.’” When you read, ‘In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful’ it is then not an offense not to read: ‘I seek refuge with Allah against Satan, the condemned one.’ When you read, ‘In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful’, it protects you from things between the sky and earth.”
